claim_bullets.bullet_text: It has a certain important meaning in its own right, and it's also an excellent excuse to learn about functions that make functions, environments, variables, and more. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.41-42)
The second x , the one in => x , is not an argument, it's an expression referring to a variable . (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.41-42)
Arguments and variables work the same way whether we're talking about (x) => (y) => x or just plain (x) => x . (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.41-42)

source_record_text: Chapter on variables and bindings from 'JavaScript Allongé'.
